audit_items.proof_trace: The only `free` calls in rio.c are in `rioFreeFdset` (L281-283), which frees `fds`, `state`, and `buf` sequentially then returns immediately without further pointer access. SDS mutations in `rioFdsetWrite` (L178) and `rioBufferWrite` (L62) always assign `sdscatlen`'s return value back to the struct member before any subsequent dereference, so no stale pointer is ever accessed. No double-free is possible since each field is freed exactly once in `rioFreeFdset` and no other function in the file calls free.
